novel_features.name: Autonomous soup-to-nuts hire
novel_features.description: Say the job and the date; goat searches, ranks by review quality and honest all-in price, and checks out against the card on file with no prompt.
novel_features.rationale: Only a client-side agent can walk the whole TaskRabbit funnel and commit checkout unattended; the app forces a human through every step.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants a task done, not a Tasker shortlist to review.

novel_features.name: Verified cancel (the safety gate)
novel_features.description: Cancels a booking and confirms it landed by re-reading status, reporting whether it was inside the free window and any fee.
novel_features.rationale: Requires reading back booking state after the mutation; a fire-and-forget cancel would defeat the whole hands-off safety model.
novel_features.why_it_matters: This is the undo that makes autonomous checkout tolerable; always available after a hire.

novel_features.name: Spend cap
novel_features.description: Refuses to check out when the computed all-in total exceeds a configurable ceiling, printing the total and the cap.
novel_features.rationale: Bounds blast radius on unattended checkout without adding a per-hire human prompt.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use --max-total (or the config default) to cap autonomous spend before any booking is placed.

novel_features.name: All-in price
novel_features.description: Folds TaskRabbit's hidden service (~15%) and trust-and-support (5-15%) fees into the displayed hourly rate, honoring the CA/MA service-fee-only rule.
novel_features.rationale: The app hides fees until checkout; the CLI computes the honest effective rate client-side and makes it the default price everywhere.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Every price surface (search, compare, best, hire, spend) shows the real all-in rate, not the teaser.

novel_features.name: Cross-source dispatch
novel_features.description: Routes a plain-language task to Magic (remote-doable) or TaskRabbit (in-person) by task shape, with a --via override.
novel_features.rationale: Only a unified binary over both human networks can pick the right one from the task alone.
novel_features.example: human-goat-pp-cli dispatch "call the dentist and reschedule my cleaning"
novel_features.why_it_matters: Say what you want done and let goat pick the human network; force it with --via taskrabbit|magic.

novel_features.name: Cross-source spend analytics
novel_features.description: SQL over local booking, invoice, and Magic-task history by category, tasker, source, or month, using true effective all-in $/hr for TaskRabbit.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local store joining bookings, invoices, and remote tasks that only exist together offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers where the money went across both human networks with fees folded in.

novel_features.name: Availability watch
novel_features.description: Polls recommendations for a category and date (optionally a favorite or a rate ceiling) and alerts when a match opens.
novel_features.rationale: The app has no watch primitive; polling the recommendations endpoint from the store enables it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when nothing good is available now and you want the first qualifying slot.

novel_features.name: Unified in-flight inbox
novel_features.description: One list of every in-flight task across TaskRabbit bookings and Magic requests, joined on the common task model and sorted by state.
novel_features.rationale: Only a unified store joining two human networks can show all pending work in one place; each backend only knows its own.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for a cross-source view of everything in flight; use 'tasks list' for TR-only and 'track <id>' for one Magic request.

narrative.headline: Hire real humans from the terminal — autonomous TaskRabbit checkout with a verified undo, plus Magic remote errands, in one agent-native binary.
narrative.value_prop: goat unifies two human networks behind a common task model: TaskRabbit for in-person local labor and Magic for remote errands. Its headline is hands-off checkout on TaskRabbit against the card on file — searched, ranked by honest all-in price and review quality, and booked with no prompt — made safe by a spend cap and a cancel command that verifies the cancellation actually landed.
narrative.auth_narrative: TaskRabbit auth is cookie replay from a logged-in Chrome session: run `human-goat-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to lift the session + XSRF-TOKEN cookies. Never a programmatic password login (the login form is reCAPTCHA-gated). Magic uses an x-api-key read from $MAGIC_API_KEY or ~/.magic/api_key.

quickstart.comment: check both backends and resolve the account metro before doing anything
see the task templates available in your metro
find the best-value available Tasker with all-in pricing
render the confirm summary without booking; drop --dry-run to check out for real

troubleshoots.fix: run `human-goat-pp-cli auth login --chrome` while logged into taskrabbit.com in Chrome

troubleshoots.symptom: hire refuses with a spend-cap message
troubleshoots.fix: raise --max-total or pick a cheaper Tasker; the printed all-in total exceeded the cap
the human's answer arrives in the conversation, not result; read it with `track <id>` which surfaces the conversation tail

narrative.when_to_use: Use goat when an agent should get a real-world task done end to end — hire a mover, assemble furniture, make a phone call, book something online — rather than surfacing options for a human to finish. It is strongest for autonomous TaskRabbit hiring with a safety net and for dispatching remote errands to Magic.
anti_triggers: Do not use goat to edit the payment method on file — the card is managed in the TaskRabbit app.
Do not use goat for programmatic password login to either service — auth is cookie/api-key only.
Do not use goat to move money outside a task booking — no transfers, payouts, or standalone tipping.

recipes.title: Autonomous hire with a spend cap
recipes.explanation: Searches, ranks by all-in price x reviews, checks out under the cap, and prints the booking id, Tasker, time, and total with no prompt.

recipes.title: Undo a booking and verify
recipes.explanation: Cancels, re-reads status, and reports cancelled plus whether it was inside the free window and any fee.

recipes.title: Dispatch a remote errand
recipes.command: human-goat-pp-cli call 5209076052 "when does the jewelry store open"
recipes.explanation: Sends a phone-call task to Magic and returns a request id to track; the answer comes back in the conversation.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose payload for an agent
recipes.explanation: Returns only the fields an agent needs from a deeply nested Tasker list instead of the full payload.

recipes.title: Where did the money go
recipes.explanation: Splits TaskRabbit vs Magic totals from the local store, with TaskRabbit rows using true all-in effective rate.

trigger_phrases: book someone to assemble my furniture
